patches.hypothesis: The issue arises because when the mean, variance, or geometric mean implementations are overridden, they are not properly incremented within the addValue method. This leads to NaN values being returned since the overridden implementations are not updated with new data points.

The issue arises because the default implementations of meanImpl, varianceImpl, and geoMeanImpl are not being used when they are not overridden. When overridden, the custom implementations are not being updated correctly in the addValue method.

The issue arises because the custom implementations of mean, variance, and geometric mean are not being incremented when they are set to instances of Mean, Variance, and GeometricMean respectively. The conditions checking if the implementations are not instances of these classes are incorrect, causing the custom implementations to never be incremented.
